titleStmt.title: SII 36.215: original edition by P Shanmugam
No. 215. (A.R. No. 215 of 1910.) PĀḌI (Tiruvalidāyam), SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT. On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruvallīśvara temple.

summary.p: (Plavaṅga, Mithuna, śu., 12, Friday, Anilam (Anusham) = A.D. 1427, June 6, Friday.).

A sarvamānya gift of tax, (iḍaṅgai vari) was made in favour of the temple of Tiruvallidāyam in Ambattūr-nāḍu in Puḻal-kōṭṭam alias Vikramachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, a subdivision of Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. The tax, was levied on the iḍaṅgai classes and vaṉṉiyars from the many paṟṟus of the Chandragiri region. The income from the tax shall be utilized for conducting the hunting (tiruvēṭṭai) festival in the temple. The record was signed by Iḍaṅgai Mīkāmavēḷāṉ.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīmaṉ makaāmaṇṭalīcuraṉ
pāṣaikkut tappuvarāya
r kaṇṭaṉ mūvarāyar kaṇ
ṭaṉ pūvva pakṣa tekṣaṇa Uttirāpati
vīra śrī tevarāyaṟkkuc cellāniṉṟa pila
vaṅka varuṣattu mitaṉa nāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapak
ṣattu duvādaciyum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum pe
ṟṟa Aṉiḻattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭaco
ḻa maṇṭalattu puḻal koṭṭam
Āṉa vikramacoḻa vaḷanāṭṭu Ampattūr nāṭṭu
tiruvallitāyattu Uṭaiyār tiruvali
tāyamuṭaiya nāyiṉār śrīpaṇṭārattuk
ku cantirakiri cūḻnta pala paṟṟil Iṭaṅkaiyi
l Uḷḷārarum vaṉṉiyarum nāyaṉār tiruval
litāyamuṭaiya nāyiṉāṟku pāṭiyillirukku
m paḷḷikaḷ peril Iṭaṅkai vari Uḷḷatukku sarvva
mānya kalveṭṭikkuṭuttapaṭi Ivarkaḷ peril Iṭaṅkai varippa
ṇamuḷḷatu nāyaṉār tiruveṭṭai Eḻuntaruḷukiṟa Upa
yattukkuc cellum kuṟaivaṟuppukku
Inta Ūr Iṭaṅkai vari sarvvamānyamākavum tirume
ykāppāṟku nāṭṭil Oṉṟu pātiyum kuṭikku Oṉṟu mukkā
lum koḷḷak kaṭavarkaḷākavum Ippaṭi cammatittu kalve
ṭṭik kuṭuttom nāyaṉār tiruvallitāyamuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku
cantirakiri cūḻnta pala paṟṟil Iṭaṅkaiyil Uḷḷārum
vaṉṉiyarum Ippaṭikku cammatittut taṉmacātaṉa
m paṇṇik kuṭuttom Inta taṉmattuk
kuṭuttom Ippaṭikku Ivai Iṭaṅkai mīkāmaveḷāṉ Eḻuttu
Digital edition of SII 36.215 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
